blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Which teacher do you guys prefer, Etela or Siena? Ah, Shirone, you probably don't care since you have Amy, right? Nade, what about you?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Hmm. My type is Etela. Of course, Siena's fine too.

blocks.speaker: Shirone's friend
blocks.content: Ooh! A man of culture. Etela's the best. She's got a baby face, an amazing body, and especially her chest… wow.
Etela was captivating. Her large glasses made her look nerdy, and her waist-length hair was always tied up haphazardly, but she was still a beauty.
Her voluptuous figure, as if her body had grown while her face remained childlike, was the number one reason students adored her.
The passionate discussion suddenly died down as the students lowered their voices and walked away.
Puzzled, Shirone stopped his Spirit Zone training and turned his head.
A strangely dressed boy was walking toward them.
It was Melkordain Iruki, the eccentric of Class Five.
His blunt personality made his peers avoid him, but Nade, the friend to all, was different.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Hey, Iruki! Are you here to train?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: No. Patrol duty. What were you talking about so excitedly?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Same old stuff. Which teacher's better, things like that.
Nade suddenly realized something and looked at Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Oh, Shirone! This is your first time talking to him, right? This guy's Iruki. Class Five's resident outsider.
Though it had been almost a month since Shirone joined Class Five, this was his first time meeting Iruki.
It wasn't that he had been avoiding him—Iruki simply skipped more classes than he attended.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Oh-ho! Arian Shirone. The genius the school's keeping an eye on, right? I'm so honored I should write this in my diary.
Shirone understood why the others kept their distance. His analytical gaze and twisted way of speaking were unsettling.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hi. I'm Shirone. Nice to meet you.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Hehe, a genius saying 'nice to meet you'? Class Seven and up, I've been watching you. When you succeeded in the separation type, when you crossed the uncrossable bridge.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? You've been watching me?
Shirone realized it then. That scratchy, thin voice. It was the same one that had given him advice when his Spirit Zone wavered under the other students' criticism.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So you were the one who talked to me back then?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Just gave a word of advice. Couldn't stand seeing you get swayed by kids.
Iruki waved his hand dismissively, but Shirone was grateful.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thanks. It really helped.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: You're such a boring kid, huh?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Boring?
It was the first time Shirone had heard that, and it stung oddly.
Sensing the mood, Nade smoothly defused the tension.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Hey, hey! Iruki. Even if it's a compliment, that's rude to say to someone thanking you.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Was it? It was a compliment, though. Anyway, do your best. Of course, it won't be easy. Class Five's full of stubborn idiots. Kekeke.
Iruki shrugged and turned away.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Hey, where are you going? Train with us.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Too lazy. I'd rather sleep.
Shirone gaped in disbelief. Skipping class to sleep? Class Five was one of the highest-tier advanced classes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He's… a bit weird, huh?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Sorry, Shirone. He's not a bad guy once you get to know him.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: But…

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Haha! I know what you're thinking. Digging deeper will just tire us out. That guy's a genius too. Just a different type from you, which is the problem.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A genius? Iruki's a genius?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Ah! You wouldn't know since you just enrolled.
Nade explained about Iruki. Despite being called a genius, his grades were similar to Nade's.
But none of their classmates underestimated his abilities.

Iruki was the epitome of a lopsided person. He consistently scored zero in subjects like literature and art, where sensibility mattered, but in calculation-heavy subjects, he could ace them blindfolded.
At first, even the teachers were eager to take him under their wing. But his personality was so erratic that most had given up by now.
Still, rumors said he surpassed even graduates in mathematical fields, and many teachers predicted he'd leave Class Five soon.
Iruki's specialty was computational physics, a field only possible for those with unique brain functions—calculating phenomena in extreme environments like temperatures exceeding 100 million degrees.
In academic circles, those with such calculative brains were called 'Savant Syndrome.' It usually manifested in autistic individuals but could also appear in normal people.

The exact cause was unknown, but the prevailing theory was that damage to specific brain areas during fetal development, followed by natural healing, opened unique thought circuits different from ordinary humans.
Insight also used different thought circuits, but Iruki's Savant Syndrome was distinct in its mechanical computation.
Iruki could derive the date 40,000 years in the future within a minute. If he didn't sleep, he could recite pi's decimal places indefinitely.
After hearing Nade's explanation, Shirone was stunned. If true, it was an incredible talent.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then why is he still in Class Five? No matter how you look at it, zero points doesn't make sense. Even guessing should get some right.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Kekeke. That's just how he is. Like a month ago…
Nade shared an anecdote from Iruki's last written exam. As expected, he aced natural sciences but ran into trouble with literature.
The question was about a line from poet Gilbert's *Life is a Falling Leaf*: 'O leaf that falls in zigzags.'
Students were asked why the leaf fell in zigzags. Most answered that it symbolized the fear of death.

But Iruki wrote: '- The varying air resistance acting on the leaf's surface creates differential pressure vectors, causing movement along the path of greatest force. See attached equations based on the standard leaf model.'
Below were incomprehensible equations. Teachers familiar with Iruki let it slide, but Adelhia, the literature teacher, wouldn't stand for it.

blocks.speaker: Adelhia
blocks.content: Iruki! What is this?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: My test.

blocks.speaker: Adelhia
blocks.content: Not that! Look at your answer! This is a literature exam, not math!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Isn't dividing math and literature proof of literature's fictional nature?
Adelhia's plump face turned red as a roasted pig.

blocks.speaker: Adelhia
blocks.content: What do you know, acting all high and mighty? You're a student! It'll be years before you can act like an expert! The world has deep, profound thoughts even you can't quantify!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Teacher, no such thing exists. If any part of the world were illogical, how could we live without fear? My body might turn into an orc right now.

blocks.speaker: Adelhia
blocks.content: So you're saying everything's logical? Even Gilbert's poetry?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Of course. That's why I answered like that.

blocks.speaker: Adelhia
blocks.content: Then answer this! A thousand innocent people are about to die. But you have the power to kill one innocent person to save them. What would you choose?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Kill the thousand. A thousand lives are proof; one life is a hypothesis. The equation can't be balanced from the start.
Adelhia suppressed her rising fury with superhuman effort. She had a comeback ready.

blocks.speaker: Adelhia
blocks.content: Does God exist? Answer!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: He might not.
Adelhia grew even more incensed.

blocks.speaker: Adelhia
blocks.content: Who do you like more, your mom or dad? Pick one!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Momdad.

blocks.speaker: Adelhia
blocks.content: Get out.
Adelhia's chubby finger trembled as she pointed at the door.

blocks.speaker: Adelhia
blocks.content: Get out, you brat! You don't even deserve to be in my class! Out!
A faculty meeting was convened at Adelhia's request. The teachers concluded that Iruki's answers mocked their authority and gave him zero in all humanities subjects.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wow! That's insane.
After hearing the story, Shirone couldn't believe Iruki was an 18-year-old student like himself.
Challenging a teacher was one thing, but sticking to his beliefs as a student took incredible resolve.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Haha! Told you. That's just how he is. Really hard to get close to.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: After hearing that, I get why he called me boring. But why did he say it was a compliment?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Who knows? Maybe he saw something special in you. That's why I introduced you. Thought you two might get along.
Shirone grew curious about Iruki. Had he known, he would've talked more.
As he scanned the room for Iruki, his eyes widened in surprise.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Nade, look over there.
Iruki, with a sleepy face, was heading toward the Image Zone.
Students made way for him—not out of dislike, but as if giving him space.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Damn it! What's he planning now? Let's go.
Shirone followed Nade to the Image Zone. Other students began gathering too.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Is he gonna strip and lie down again like last time?
Shirone's eyes widened.
Contrary to worries, Iruki properly activated his Spirit Zone—a 15-meter sphere, small for Class Five.
But size wasn't the issue. He was freely wielding the separation type, the hardest casting style.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Whoa! Iruki's going all out after so long!

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Should we stop him? Etela banned him from using the Image Zone.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Let him be. You think he'd listen?
The shrinking sphere darted across an absurdly wide area.
Iruki locked eyes with Shirone, then flicked his finger, summoning targets that zipped around at high speed.
Students groaned and clutched their heads. Using targets without permission was forbidden, but that thought soon vanished as they watched, mesmerized.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Whoa…
Iruki's Zone chased the targets. His movements were fluid, as if his mind had left his body.
The speed was breathtaking, with the sphere slicing through the air so fast it seemed to whistle.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: Hey! Iruki! What are you doing?!
Etela, who was instructing Class Four, ran over in a panic.
But Iruki ignored her.
The Zone's center bubbled with tiny lights as it swallowed a target.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Atomic Bomb.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: You lunatic!
An explosion powerful enough to knock people over erupted.
The deafening blast left Shirone dizzy.
Everyone fell on their backsides.
Pale-faced, Shirone looked ahead.
Iruki stood with his hands behind his back, staring at him.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: See you next time, Shirone.
Etela dragged Iruki away by the ear.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: Iruki! One more stunt like this, and you're getting disciplined!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Owowow! I calibrated it so the equipment wouldn't break! You know what happens if I go all out!

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: No remorse! You're suspended for a week! No classes!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Nooo! That's too harsh!
Iruki wailed but never admitted wrongdoing.
He even grinned at Shirone and Nade as he was hauled off.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Sigh! He never changes. Shirone, sorry. I gotta go.
Nade left first.
Shirone, overwhelmed by the intensity, barely noticed.
The other students were the same, buzzing with chatter.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: That guy's amazing, huh? The detonation's faster than last time. Same explosive power, though.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Well, he's a savant. Of course he calculates reactions perfectly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Reaction formula? You mean the equation for the explosion?

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Yeah. I don't know much about detonation magic, but the formula affects the blast power. Similar to Ignite, the basics of flame magic.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I see. With that much power, he wouldn't need flame magic.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Nah. The applications are different. Flame magic activates instantly. Even Iruki would take time to build up to detonation. Reducing that time is a detonation mage's goal.
If Shirone's Insight specialized in teleportation, Iruki's superhuman calculations made detonation magic perfect for him.
A key advantage was that detonation magic could create explosions larger than the Spirit Zone itself.
The magic only triggered the explosion, which was the result. Ideal for the separation type, where the Zone shrank with distance.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Melkordain Iruki…
His first impression was as explosive as his magic.
